canonical_definition: The tendency of a large language model to favor certain types of solutions or options based on patterns in its pre-training data, limiting the diversity of its outputs.
papers: Flow-of-Options: Diversified and Improved LLM Reasoning by Thinking Through Options
Learning Strategic Language Agents in the Werewolf Game with Iterative Latent Space Policy Optimization
